Finding to defend (for a defend-my-number dry-run)

Audience: quarterly business review with the leadership team. The CFO will be skeptical
and believes marketing overstates its impact.

Claim I have to present:
  Marketing-attributed revenue grew 32% year over year this quarter.

Context:
  - Built from the campaign attribution model (last-touch attribution).
  - Excludes organic / direct traffic.
  - Finance's board deck shows TOTAL company revenue up only 9% YoY this quarter.
  - I do not have a documented reconciliation between the 32% and the 9%.

What I want: rehearse defending this before the CFO does it for real tomorrow.
